items.title: How to Design and Code with Claude Code and Figma MCP in 50 Min | Felix Lee
items.description: Felix is an expert at using Claude Code and Figma MCP to design and build apps. Watch as he demos how you can build apps ...
extracted_patterns: Start with personal projects before client work - provides experimental sandbox for learning Claude Code workflow
Use Figma MCP to automatically convert design components to editable SVG vectors (not screenshots)
Integrate Claude Code with external data sources (Supabase RAG) to build AI chat with source attribution
Chain multiple Claude Code runs: generate image â†’ convert to dark mode â†’ integrate into design system
Build live data visualizations by combining Claude Code frontend with real API data (booking platforms, community metrics)
Use Claude's image understanding to create theme variants from single source image
Create portfolio projects that demonstrate specific tool integrations (Figma MCP, Claude API, databases)
Leverage Figma MCP to automatically populate component properties and create production-ready design tokens
Combine design iteration in Figma with code generation in Claude Code in tight feedback loop

items.title: Claude Code + Kaparthy’s AutoResearch = GOD MODE
items.description: ALL Systems: https://bit.ly/4kol0y5 Type with your voice: https://glaido.com 🎙️ My Goofy pod: https://bit.ly/46nLQ3U ...
extracted_patterns: Iterative Improvement Loop: Generate â†’ Deploy â†’ Harvest Data â†’ Analyze Performance â†’ Iterate (cycle frequency configurable: 2min-daily-custom)
Control/Challenger Testing: Maintain baseline control variant; pit AI-generated challenger variants against it; promote winner to new baseline (A/B testing automation)
Compound Learning Cycle: Each iteration builds on previous; log all experiments so they're never repeated; improvement compounds daily (1% daily â‰ˆ 300x yearly)
Version Handoff Pattern: V1 gathers research/context â†’ V2 optimizes and enhances â†’ V3 continues; each version receives playbook of prior learnings
Objective Metrics Only: Use measurable, non-subjective metrics (conversion %, pass/fail, numeric scores); reject subjective metrics (feels good, has vibe)
Three-Component Requirements: (1) Something to change (variant generation), (2) Metric to measure (objective outcome), (3) Result reader (data harvesting/analysis)
Experiment Logging as Asset: Maintain complete log of all tried variants and their outcomes; use to avoid repeated failures and guide new experiments

items.title: 9 months of Claude Code Lessons in 19 minutes
items.description: Here are 8 lessons I've learned about Claude Code after using it every day for 9 months! Join the Vibe Coding Academy: ...
extracted_patterns: Use VS Code extension over CLI or Cursor for best reliability and UX
Keep Claude Code workflows simple - avoid over-feature usage
Let Sonnet 4.5 handle complexity - it finds docs and auto-delegates to sub-agents
Don't manually orchestrate 100 sub-agents; use auto-delegation instead
Avoid building per-scenario sub-agent setups; let the model decide when to use them
Minimize slash commands usage (mainly use /clear, /new-conversation)
Claude Code auto-finds documentation for new APIs without requiring MCPs
Build full-scale apps with simple workflows rather than complex orchestration
